questions.question: What is Vitest?
options: A) A TypeScript compiler
B) A fast unit testing framework built on top of Vite
C) A visual testing tool for UI components
D) A code formatter

questions.question: What does the 'describe' function do in Vitest?
options: A) Describes the project structure
B) Groups related test cases together into a test suite
C) Describes the expected output
D) Creates documentation for tests

questions.question: What does the 'it' (or 'test') function do?
options: A) Iterates over test data
B) Defines a single test case
C) Initializes test fixtures
D) Imports test utilities
A) That add returns a string '5'
B) That add(2, 3) returns exactly 5
C) That add is defined
D) That 2 + 3 is truthy

questions.question: What is the difference between .toBe() and .toEqual()?
options: A) No difference
B) toBe uses strict reference equality (===), toEqual performs deep structural equality
C) toBe is for strings, toEqual is for numbers
D) toEqual is deprecated

questions.question:
What does this test assert?

expect({ a: 1, b: 2 }).toEqual({ a: 1, b: 2 });
options: A) That the objects are the same reference
B) That the objects have the same structure and values
C) That both objects are truthy
D) That the objects have the same keys

questions.question:
Would this test pass?

expect({ a: 1 }).toBe({ a: 1 });
options: A) Yes, they have the same content
B) No, toBe checks reference equality and these are different objects
C) Yes, toBe works like toEqual for objects
D) It would throw a syntax error

questions.question: What does vi.fn() create?
options: A) A new Vitest test file
B) A mock function that tracks calls, arguments, and return values
C) A virtual file for testing
D) A fixture factory

questions.question: How do you check if a mock function was called with specific arguments?
options: A) expect(mockFn).toBe(args)
B) expect(mockFn).toHaveBeenCalledWith(arg1, arg2)
C) expect(mockFn.args).toEqual([arg1, arg2])
D) mockFn.verify(arg1, arg2)

questions.question: What does vi.fn().mockReturnValue(42) do?
options: A) Creates a function that throws 42
B) Creates a mock function that returns 42 when called
C) Sets the test timeout to 42ms
D) Asserts the function returns 42

questions.question: How do you test an async function in Vitest?
options: A) Use it.async()
B) Make the test callback async and use await
C) Wrap the test in setTimeout
D) Async functions can't be tested
A) That fetchUser is defined
B) That fetching user 1 returns an object with name 'Alice'
C) That the fetch completes in time
D) That the data is not null

questions.question: How do you test that an async function rejects?
options: A) expect(fn()).toThrow()
B) await expect(fn()).rejects.toThrow('error message')
C) expect(await fn()).toBe(Error)
D) try { await fn() } catch { pass() }

questions.question: What does .toThrow() check?
options: A) That a value is truthy
B) That a function throws an error when called
C) That a promise rejects
D) That a value is thrown away

questions.question:
How do you correctly test that a synchronous function throws?

function boom() { throw new Error('bang'); }
options: A) expect(boom()).toThrow('bang')
B) expect(() => boom()).toThrow('bang')
C) expect(boom).toBe(Error)
D) expect(boom).toThrow.toBe('bang')

questions.question: What is the purpose of edge case testing?
options: A) Testing the user interface edges
B) Testing boundary conditions and unusual inputs like empty strings, zero, null, etc.
C) Testing only the happy path
D) Testing in edge/chromium browsers

questions.question: What does code coverage measure?
options: A) How many tests pass
B) What percentage of your source code is executed during tests
C) How fast tests run
D) How many bugs are found

questions.question: How do you run Vitest with code coverage?
options: A) vitest --verbose
B) vitest --coverage
C) vitest --report
D) vitest --analyze

questions.question: What does .toContain() check?
options: A) That an object contains a key
B) That an array includes an item or a string includes a substring
C) That a value is contained in memory
D) That a mock was called

questions.question: What does beforeEach() do in a test suite?
options: A) Runs once before all tests in the file
B) Runs a setup function before each individual test in the describe block
C) Marks the previous test as complete
D) Sets up global configuration

questions.question: What is the difference between beforeEach and beforeAll?
options: A) No difference
B) beforeEach runs before every test; beforeAll runs once before all tests in the suite
C) beforeAll runs before each test; beforeEach runs once
D) beforeAll is for async, beforeEach is for sync

questions.question: What does vi.spyOn(object, 'method') do?
options: A) Deletes the method from the object
B) Creates a spy on an existing method, tracking calls while preserving original behavior
C) Creates a new method on the object
D) Converts the method to async

questions.question: What does .toHaveBeenCalledTimes(n) check?
options: A) That a function took n milliseconds
B) That a mock/spy function was called exactly n times
C) That n tests have run
D) That a loop ran n times

questions.question: What does it.skip() do?
options: A) Deletes the test
B) Skips the test so it doesn't run but remains in the suite
C) Runs the test in reverse
D) Marks the test as expected to fail

questions.question: What does .toBeTruthy() check?
options: A) That the value is exactly true
B) That the value is truthy (not false, 0, '', null, undefined, or NaN)
C) That the value is a boolean
D) That the value exists in the DOM
